lessons.title: Semantic page structure
lessons.description: Use meaningful elements to organise content.
lessons.starter:
CodeQuest News

Learning by building

Practice creates confidence.
lessons.challenge: Build a semantic article page with header, main and article elements.

lessons.title: Links and images
lessons.description: Connect pages and add meaningful media.
lessons.starter:
Travel guide

Read the full guide
lessons.challenge: Create an accessible image and external link.

lessons.title: Forms and labels
lessons.description: Collect data with accessible controls.
lessons.starter:
<form>
  <label for="email">Email</label>
  <input id="email" type="email" required>
  <button>Subscribe</button>
</form>
lessons.challenge: Build an email subscription form with a label and required validation.

lessons.title: Accessibility foundations
lessons.description: Use landmarks, labels and useful alternative text.
lessons.starter:
Skip to content

Accessible page
lessons.challenge: Create a page with useful navigation and a main landmark.

modules.title: Stage 2 · CSS Foundations
modules.description: Style interfaces with layout, hierarchy and responsive behaviour.

lessons.title: Selectors and cascade
lessons.description: Target elements and reason about precedence.
lessons.starter:
Welcome

Start learning.
lessons.challenge: Style a card and its heading with class and descendant selectors.

lessons.title: Box model
lessons.description: Control spacing, borders and sizing.
lessons.starter: Content
lessons.challenge: Create a fixed-width box whose padding stays inside the declared width.

lessons.title: Flexbox and Grid
lessons.description: Build reliable one- and two-dimensional layouts.
lessons.starter:
<section class="grid">
  <article>One</article><article>Two</article><article>Three</article>
</section>
<style>
.grid { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(3,1fr); gap:16px; }
</style>
lessons.challenge: Create a three-column grid with equal columns and gaps.

lessons.title: Responsive design
lessons.description: Adapt layouts across screen sizes.
lessons.starter:
<section class="cards"><article>A</article><article>B</article></section>
<style>
.cards { display:grid; grid-template-columns:repeat(2,1fr); gap:16px; }
@media (max-width:600px) { .cards { grid-template-columns:1fr; } }
</style>
lessons.challenge: Make a two-column layout collapse to one column on small screens.

modules.title: Stage 3 · JavaScript Foundations
modules.description: Add behaviour with variables, functions and collections.

lessons.title: Variables and types
lessons.description: Store values and inspect their types.
lessons.challenge: Calculate and display a total when the button is clicked.

lessons.title: Functions
lessons.description: Create reusable behaviour.
lessons.challenge: Create a reusable greeting function and call it from an event.

lessons.title: Arrays and objects
lessons.description: Transform collections of structured data.
lessons.challenge: Render an array of course objects into a list.

lessons.title: Errors and debugging
lessons.description: Use browser feedback to find and fix problems.
lessons.challenge: Catch a JSON parsing error and show a useful message.

modules.title: Stage 4 · Browser Development
modules.description: Work with the DOM, events, storage and asynchronous APIs.

lessons.title: DOM manipulation
lessons.description: Create and update interface elements.
lessons.challenge: Add a new list item each time the button is clicked.

lessons.title: Events and forms
lessons.description: Respond to user input safely.
lessons.challenge: Handle a form submission without reloading the page.

lessons.title: Fetch and async JavaScript
lessons.description: Load remote data and handle failure.
lessons.challenge: Fetch JSON data and display either the result or an error.

lessons.title: Local storage
lessons.description: Persist small amounts of browser state.
lessons.challenge: Save and restore a note using localStorage.

modules.title: Stage 5 · Professional Frontend
modules.description: Build maintainable, tested and deployable frontend applications.

lessons.title: JavaScript modules
lessons.description: Separate code into focused units.
lessons.starter:
Module design

Split data, rendering and event handling into separate functions.
lessons.challenge: Describe how you would separate data, rendering and event logic.

lessons.title: Component thinking
lessons.description: Design reusable UI structures.
lessons.starter:
Name

Role
lessons.challenge: Create a reusable profile-card structure with clear content slots.

lessons.title: Frontend testing
lessons.description: Validate behaviour and accessibility.
lessons.starter:
<button id="toggle" aria-expanded="false">Menu</button><nav id="menu" hidden>Links</nav>
<script>
const b=document.querySelector('#toggle'),m=document.querySelector('#menu');b.onclick=()=>{const open=b.getAttribute('aria-expanded')==='true';b.setAttribute('aria-expanded',String(!open));m.hidden=open;};
</script>
lessons.challenge: Create a menu toggle whose state is testable through aria-expanded.

lessons.title: Performance and deployment
lessons.description: Optimise assets and verify production readiness.
lessons.starter:
Production checklist

  • Optimised images
  • Accessible interactions
  • No console errors
  • Responsive layout
lessons.challenge: Create a frontend production-readiness checklist.
